WATERSPOUT MOVED ONSHORE JUST WEST OF KALMUS IN SOUTHEAST BARNSTABLE. 91 MPH GUST WAS RECORDED. EF1 TORNADO WITH MAX WINDS OF 110 MPH MOVED NE ACROSS YARMOUTH AND LIFTED IN SOUTH YARMOUTH. DAMAGE WAS DISCONTINUOUS. NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. ROOF OF MOTEL RIPPED OFF.
ROOF BLOWN OFF A SECTION OF CAPE SANDS INN.

A BRIEF, NARROW TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN WEST YARMOUTH AT THE SAME TIME AS ANOTHER TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND JUST TO ITS NORTH. THIS TORNADO WAS ONLY 50 YARDS WIDE AND ONE-QUARTER MILE IN LENGTH. IT STRETCHED FROM YACHT AVE. AND SCHOONER ST. TO EGG HARBOR RD. JUST EAST OF PINE CONE DR. MANY PINE TREES SNAPPED. MAX WIND GUSTS 90 MPH.

CONFIRMED TORNADO BY NWS SURVEY TEAM. DAMAGE FROM THE TORNADO BEGAN IN YARMOUTH AND ENDED IN HARWICH. THE DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY BOTH A TORNADO AND STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE WITH THE STRONGEST WIND ESTIMATED TO BE 110MPH, AN EF1. THE GREATEST AMOUNT OF DAMAGE APPEARS TO HAVE OCCURRED IN THE TOWN OF HARWICH. THERE WAS EXTENSIVE TREE AND POWERLINE DAMAGE. MOST OF THE TOWN OF HARWICH LOST POWER DUE TO THE STORM.

SAME CELL THAT PRODUCED THE BARNSTABLE-YARMOUTH TORNADO CAUSED ANOTHER TORNADO IN HARWICH THAT WAS ON THE GROUND FROM 1210 PM TO 1215 PM. EF1 RANKING WITH MAX WIND GUSTS OF 110 MPH. STARTED JUST SOUTH OF PARALLEL ST., MOVED NORTHEAST ACROSS HARWICH CENTER, THEN LIFTED NEAR QUEEN ANNE ROAD IN EAST HARWICH. 150+ TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED.

EF-0, 65 MPH, MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 60 YDS, PATH LENGTH 1/4 MILE, DURATION - ONLY 1 MINUTE. DAMAGE FOCUSED FROM PROSPECT FARM ROAD TO SEA MEADOW DRIVE. SNAPPED TREES AND EYEWITNESSES REPORTED LAWN FURNITURE AND TREE LIMBS LOFTED INTO THE AIR. ALSO SEVERAL VIDEOS CONFIRMING A FUNNEL TOUCHING DOWN.
QUARTER HAIL REPORTED FROM 342 PM THROUGH 344 PM.
